claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many and what are their ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'm responsible for. Alex is 17 and Blake is 18. They both live with me at home. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have two children - Alex (17) and Blake (18) living with you at home.

Since both of your children are over 16, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training. Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training.

**Could you tell me whether Alex and Blake are currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, college courses, or approved training programs)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in college doing A-levels. But Blake has finished school - he's not studying anymore or doing any training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Just to confirm Blake's situation: **Is Blake currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work? Also, is he claiming any benefits in his own name, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't working at all right now, and he's not claiming any benefits in his own name either. He's just at home at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me also confirm one final detail:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

**For Alex (17):** ✅ **You ARE eligible for Child Benefit**
Since Alex is 17 and currently in college doing A-levels, which counts as approved education, you qualify for Child Benefit. The rules allow Child Benefit to continue for children under 20 who stay in approved education or training.

**For Blake (18):** ❌ **You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit**
Unfortunately, Blake has finished school and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 only continues if they remain in approved education or training. Since Blake is not studying or in training, you cannot claim Child Benefit for him.

**What you should know:**
- You can claim Child Benefit for Alex as long as he continues in approved education and remains under 20
- If Blake decides to return to education or approved training in the future and is still under 20, you could become eligible to claim for him again
- Please note: if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit situation?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is currently in college doing A-levels, which counts as approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is under 20 (age 17) and is in approved education (A-levels at college), you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 18 years old and has finished school. Blake is not currently in approved education or training, not working, and not claiming any benefits. Under Child Benefit rules, eligibility can continue for children aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake. Note: You could apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if Blake (as a 16 or 17 year old) registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but Blake is 18, so this exception does not apply.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for two children living at home: Alex (17) and Blake (18). Alex is eligible because he is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels). Blake is not eligible because he is over 16, not in approved education or training, and does not meet any other qualifying criteria.

Blake is not eligible because he is over 16, not in approved education or training, and does not meet any other qualifying criteria.
